content.claim: Hungary exhibits large socio-economic performance gaps (53-point PISA gap between children of graduates vs. non-graduates, vs. 33-point EU average) and highest EU segregation of advantaged/disadvantaged students into different schools
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Segregation correlates with early tracking but does not prove causation
Cannot distinguish segregation driven by age-12 gimnázium selection from primary-school choice mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
